summary:
Brad demonstrated his secure OpenClaw deployment infrastructure on AWS — each team member gets their own instance at $12/month with no SSH, no public IP, SSM session manager access, shared IAM role profiles, and Okta SSO with MFA. He's planning a one-click deploy script via AWS CloudShell for the Tuesday all-hands. The architecture enables team agents to communicate securely while keeping individual instances isolated. Brad sees this potentially becoming a product itself — a secure deployment platform for AI agents, possibly replacing Chief Sidekick.

Brad showcased two active OpenClaw projects: Legion (lead gen pipeline) that uses heartbeats to scan Reddit and job postings for prospects (found Zook posting for a help center intern), and a content pipeline that generates 6 daily content ideas with scripts, metadata, source links, and AI-generated thumbnails via Nano Banana. He's created 2 videos from it already on his Digital at Scale YouTube channel. Brad also switched from EXA to Fire Crawl for Camelo's domain research — reducing analysis time from 3 minutes to 11 seconds at dramatically lower cost ($120 vs $1,700 for the same volume). He ran a script finding 13,000 domains that had inaccurate results from the original EXA approach.

They discussed prep for the Max conversation from the earlier AAI sync. Key talking points: if SBI doesn't close, AAI can't continue funding anyone; Max would need to go hourly or accept a ~25% base reduction with equity (~10%); everyone's hours must be billable; revenue share up to 70% for mini-app creators. Adam emphasized that Last Rev can't keep investing in AAI without returns, and even the $400-500K spent on the framework didn't yield 10X returns. Brad submitted a large PR (135 files) cleaning up the Last Rev marketing website — removing dead code, fixing 320 instances of 'any' types, security issues, and duplicate code. Adam noted some concerns about use-client removal but agreed to review.
keyDiscussionPoints: Secure AWS deployment for OpenClaw — $12/month per instance, Okta SSO, no SSH/public IP
Legion lead gen pipeline scanning Reddit, job postings for prospects
Content pipeline generating 6 daily ideas with scripts and thumbnails
Fire Crawl replacing EXA — 11 seconds vs 3 minutes, $120 vs $1,700 for domain research
13,000 domains re-analyzed with Fire Crawl for Camelo
Answer Engine pricing spreadsheet with credit scenarios and tier plans
Prep for Max compensation discussion — hourly or reduced base + equity
Last Rev marketing website PR — 135 files, type fixes, dead code removal
Lead gen strategies: scan for Contentful CDN usage, check domain age via Wayback Machine
Potential for OpenClaw deployment platform as a product
decisions: Fire Crawl adopted for all domain research going forward
Waive Camelo's re-analysis costs as goodwill gesture
OpenClaw deployment to be presented at Tuesday all-hands
Billing minimum of 20 hours/week per person to be implemented
Mini-app revenue share up to 70% to incentivize team

actionItems.action: Prepare OpenClaw deployment tutorial for Tuesday all-hands
Record and publish OpenClaw AWS deploy video
Review and merge Last Rev marketing website PR (135 files)
Fix blog route bugs in marketing website
Write 50 blog posts via OpenClaw for Last Rev site
Send content pipeline config files to Max
Have Krista draft Diligent scope/invoice document
Talk to Adam Cohen (Diligent) about next SOW
Integrate AWS Secrets Manager into OpenClaw deploy (replace .env)
Set up Last Rev Google service account for OpenClaw Drive access

followUp: Last Rev marketing website PR review and blog bug fixes
Answer Engine pricing model finalization with Stripe
